frameworks.name: Bayesian inference under uncertainty
frameworks.one_line: balancing new observations against existing knowledge

core_question: How should successor representations be learned to handle uncertainty and switches of context?
key_assumptions: learning should optimally weight new evidence vs prior knowledge
multiple task-specific SRs can coexist and be selected
manipulations: uncertainty in new observations vs existing knowledge
tasks requiring contextual memory and generalisation
key_findings: prediction-error learning rule for SRs that balances uncertainty optimally
multi-context extension maintains several task-specific SRs
context inferred online from prediction accuracy
model captures animal behaviour on contextual memory tasks
interpretations: probabilistic SR provides an intermediate RL strategy adaptive to context
uncertainty-weighted SR learning explains hippocampal-supported flexible prediction
